review_summary: FY2026 Q3 guidance materially improves the thesis: paid Copilot enterprise penetration moved above 12%, Azure AI demand remains above 30%, and Microsoft now guides to slower AI infrastructure CapEx growth. This converts the prior Copilot and CapEx concerns from watch/weakness into reinforcement, raising confidence and supporting a continued hold while awaiting the next earnings confirmation.

changed_assumptions.summary: Reinforced further: Azure AI demand remains above 30%, supporting the growth threshold and lowering near-term invalidation risk.
Materially improved: paid Copilot enterprise penetration moved above 12%, clearing the prior adoption hurdle and strengthening the AI distribution premium case.
Improved: Microsoft is guiding to slower AI infrastructure CapEx growth, reducing the risk that spend remains a pure cost drag.

action_rule_delta.summary: Condition is now met on Copilot penetration (>10%) and CapEx growth moderation; however, action remains contingent on earnings confirmation before adding.
Less likely to trigger because CapEx is moderating rather than re-accelerating while monetization is improving.
No change; exit conditions are not approached given Azure growth remains above 30% and flagship cloud access is not impaired.

new_events.title: Microsoft says paid Copilot enterprise penetration moved above 12% in FY2026 Q3 guidance.
Microsoft guides to slower AI infrastructure CapEx growth while Azure AI demand remains above 30%.

material_events.title: Microsoft says paid Copilot enterprise penetration moved above 12% in FY2026 Q3 guidance.
Microsoft guides to slower AI infrastructure CapEx growth while Azure AI demand remains above 30%.
